questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given italicize word. Her eyes were filled with compassion as she offered food to the hungry child on the street.
questions.explanation: A)Compassion(noun) – Empathy, kindness, mercy, sympathy, benevolenceकरुणा Antonym:Apathy(noun) – Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern; indifference, detachment, coldnessउदासीनता •Empathy– Ability to understand and share feelings.सहानुभूति •Mercy– Leniency, compassion shown to offenders.दया •Kindness– Being nice or considerate.दयालुता

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words underlined in the given sentence The ancient city faced complete obliteration after the devastating earthquake, leaving no trace of its former grandeur.
questions.explanation: C)Annihilation(noun) – Complete destruction or obliteration, especially when nothing is left behind. संहार Elimination(noun) – The act of removing or getting rid of something, often gradually or selectively. समाप्ति Abolition(noun) – The act of formally putting an end to a system, practice, or institution. उन्मूलन Purge(noun) – The removal or cleansing of undesirable elements, often from a place or organization. शुद्धिकरण

questions.question: Select the grammatically correct sentence from the options given
options: Neither a boy nor a mother could identify the man.
Neither the boy nor a mother could identify a man.
Neither the boy nor the mother could identify the man.
Neither a boy nor the mother could identify a man.
questions.explanation: C) 'Neither the boy nor the mother could identify the man. 'Neither the boy nor the mother could identify the man.' सही है क्योंकि 'Neither ... nor' का प्रयोग करते समय parallel nouns का प्रयोग आवश्यक होता है, और यहाँ 'the boy' तथा 'the mother' स्पष्ट रूप से विशिष्ट व्यक्तियों को संदर्भित करते हैं। जैसे— Neither the boy nor the mother could identify the man. • 'Neither the boy nor the mother could identify the man.' is correct because 'Neither … nor' requires parallel nouns, and 'the boy' and 'the mother' properly refer to specific people; Like— Neither the boy nor the mother could identify the man.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given italicize word. He was always mindful of his words when speaking to elders, never offending anyone.
questions.explanation: A)Mindful(adjective) – Aware, conscious, attentive, careful, thoughtfulजागरूक / सजग Antonym:Oblivious(adjective) – Unaware, ignorant, inattentive, heedlessबेपरवाह / अनजान •Attentive– Paying attention.ध्यान देने वाला •Careful– Cautious to avoid harm.सावधान •Conscious– Aware and responsive.सचेत

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. He was caught by the police on the spot. B. Anil bought a car last week. C. Unfortunately, the car met with an accident today. D. The accident took place due to the carelessness of his driver.
questions.explanation: B) BCDA Bstarts the paragraph by introducing the subject"Anil"and the action"bought a car,"setting the context for the events that follow. CfollowsBlogically, as it introduces a contrasting event ("Unfortunately, the car met with an accident today") that relates directly to the car mentioned inB. Dcomes afterCbecause it explains thereasonfor the accident ("due to the carelessness of his driver"), providing a cause-effect relationship. Aconcludes the paragraph by stating the consequence ("He was caught by the police on the spot"), which is a direct result of the accident described earlier. In Hindi: Bparagraph की शुरुआत करता है, क्योंकि यह subject"Anil"और उसके action"bought a car"को introduce करता है, जिससे आगे की घटनाओं का संदर्भ बनता है। C,Bके बाद आता है क्योंकि यह एक विपरीत घटना ("car met with an accident") को बताता है, जोBमें खरीदी गई कार से जुड़ी है। D,Cके बाद आता है क्योंकि यह accident काकारण("due to the carelessness of his driver") बताता है, जिससे cause-effect relationship स्पष्ट होती है। Aparagraph को conclude करता है, क्योंकि यह accident कापरिणाम("caught by the police") बताता है, जो पहले की घटनाओं का logical अंत है।

questions.question: Select the option that gives the most appropriate meaning of the underlined word. The women of this country never questioned anything. Why are they so credulous?
options: Willing to believe promptly without adequate evidence
Indifferent to the basic necessities of life
Reluctant to face the difficulties of life with courage
questions.explanation: A)Credulous(adjective): Having a tendency to believe things too easily without sufficient evidence; gullible; विश्वासशील Suspicious(adjective): Doubtful, distrustful; संदेही Indifferent(adjective): Unconcerned, apathetic; उदासीन Reluctant(adjective): Unwilling, hesitant; अनिच्छुक

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank. All my efforts went in vain. The project on which I was working for the last two months has failed and we are _________________.
questions.explanation: A)Back to square one(idiom) - Starting over because a previous effort has failed or not produced results.पुनः प्रारंभ से

questions.question: In the given sentence, one of the four underlined words is incorrectly spelt. Identify the INCORRECTLY spelt word. She dabbled in drawing and painting, but scalpture was her favourite medium.
questions.explanation: D) The correct spelling of the incorrectly spelt word'scalpture'is'sculpture', which means "the art of making two- or three-dimensional representative or abstract forms, especially by carving stone or wood or by casting metal or plaster" (मूर्ति बनाना, शिल्पकला).

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. He behaves if he were a king
questions.explanation: C) 'behaves as if' का प्रयोग 'behaves if' के स्थान पर होगा क्योंकि 'as if' का प्रयोग तब किया जाता है जब कोई स्थिति वास्तविक न हो और काल्पनिक परिस्थिति को व्यक्त करना हो; जैसे— He behaves as if he knew everything. 'behaves as if' will be used instead of 'behaves if' because 'as if' is used when the situation is imaginary or hypothetical; Like— He behaves as if he knew everything.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank. Choosing not to pursue a degree in computer engineering was ________, as the market for them is extremely limited nowadays
questions.explanation: D)a blessing in disguise(idiom) – something that at first seems bad or unfortunate but results in something good happening later.अशुभ के रूप में शुभ

questions.question: Identify the word with a spelling error in the following sentence. Select the option that correctly rectifies the error. In Shakespeare’s play ‘Hamlet’, Hamlet’s flaw is defined as procrustineition
questions.explanation: D) The correct spelling is ‘Procrastination’which means "the action of delaying or postponing something" (किसी कार्य को टालने की क्रिया).

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words underlined in the given sentence His decision to hike up the treacherous mountain at night, without proper gear, was contrary to reason or common sense.
questions.explanation: A)Preposterous(adjective) – Contrary to reason or common sense; utterly absurd or ridiculous. बेतुका Sane(adjective) – Having a sound mind; reasonable and sensible. समझदार Shrewd(adjective) – Having sharp judgment, especially in practical matters. चतुर Sagacious(adjective) – Showing keen mental discernment and good judgment; wise. विवेकशील

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Some people are in the habit of sharing________ and bull story on unsuitable occasions.
questions.explanation: C) Cock and bull story(phrase) – An unbelievable tale that is intended to deceive; a tall tale. झूठी कहानी, झूठ

questions.question: Select the best express synonym of the given underlined word. The organization decided to employ new strategies to increase its market share.
questions.explanation: C) Employ(verb) – Use, Utilize, Apply, Engage, Deploy उपयोगकरना Synonym:Utilize(verb) – to make use of, to employ something effectively. उपयोग में लाना Permanent(adjective) – Lasting, enduring, forever. स्थायी Enduring(adjective) – Lasting, durable, permanent. टिकाऊ Eternal(adjective) – Everlasting, immortal, perpetual. अनंत

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. Chetan's room is clean / than Ram's room because / Chetan cleans it more often than Ram.
options: Chetan cleans it more often than Ram
questions.explanation: A) ‘clean’ के स्थान पर ‘cleaner’का use किया जाएगा क्योंकि यहाँ दो चीजों (Chetan का कमरा और Ram का कमरा) की तुलना की जा रही है। तुलना करने के लिए adjective का comparative form (cleaner) प्रयोग किया जाता है; जैसे—Chetan's room is cleaner than Ram's room. ‘clean’ will be replaced by ‘cleaner’because a comparison is being made between two items (Chetan's room and Ram's room). For comparison, the comparative form of the adjective (cleaner) is used; e.g.—Chetan's room is cleaner than Ram's room.

questions.question: Select the best express synonym of the given underlined word. Regular exercise can significantly stimulate your brain activity, improving both memory and concentration.
questions.explanation: A) Stimulate(verb) – Encourage, Boost, Spur, Promote, Activate प्रोत्साहितकरना Synonym:Encourage(verb) – to give support, confidence, or hope to someone. उत्साहित करना Fleeting(adjective) – Short-lived, temporary, brief, momentary, passing क्षणिक Throttle(verb) – Suppress, choke, strangle, restrain, hinder दबाना Brief(adjective) – Short, concise, fleeting, transient. संक्षिप्त

questions.question: Select the most appropriate adverb to fill in the blank. Ramees works ____________ to get through the entrance exam.
questions.explanation: C)'Hard'का use होगा क्योंकि "hard" का अर्थ होता है मेहनत से या प्रयास से। sentence में mention किया गया है कि Ramees entrance exam के लिए मेहनत करता है, इसलिए 'hard' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Different' का अर्थ है अलग, 'Early' का अर्थ है जल्दी और 'Important' का अर्थ है महत्वपूर्ण, जो इस context में सही नहीं है।

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. Her decision, / given the complex circumstances, / was too impulsively made
questions.explanation: A) 'was too impulsively made' के बदले 'was made too impulsively' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि adverb 'too impulsively' को verb 'made' के बाद रखा जाना चाहिए ताकि correct word order बना रहे; जैसे— Her decision, given the complex circumstances, was made too impulsively. 'was made too impulsively' will be used instead of 'was too impulsively made' because the adverb 'too impulsively' should follow the verb 'made' for correct word order; Like— Her decision, given the complex circumstances, was made too impulsively.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. In some cases, the problem of the lowering of the river bottom has caused the water table of the surrounding area to drop, which has adversely affected agriculture. B. Also, another problem, canalization with its extensive system of locks and navigation dams, often seriously disrupts riverine ecosystems. C. The ever-increasing exploitation of rivers has given rise to a variety of problems. D. Further, extensive commercial navigation of rivers has resulted in much artificial improvement of natural channels, including increasing the depth of the channels to permit passage of larger vessels
questions.explanation: A) CABD C begins the paragraph by introducing the subject (exploitation of rivers) and the central claim (various problems). A follows C because it provides the first specific problem (lowering of the river bottom) and its effect (drop in water table), linked by the phrase "In some cases." D comes next with another problem (commercial navigation) and its result (artificial channel improvements), connected by "Further." B concludes by adding one more problem (canalization disrupting ecosystems), introduced by "Also." Hindi Explanation: C paragraph की शुरुआत करता है क्योंकि यह subject (नदियों का बढ़ता दोहन) और central idea (विभिन्न समस्याएं) को introduce करता है। A, C के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह पहली समस्या (नदी तल का नीचे आना) और उसका प्रभाव (जल स्तर का गिरना) बताता है। D, A के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह दूसरी समस्या (व्यावसायिक नौवहन) और उसके परिणाम (नदी चैनलों में कृत्रिम बदलाव) को जोड़ता है। B paragraph को समाप्त करता है क्योंकि यह एक और समस्या (नहरों का पारिस्थितिकी को नुकसान) को जोड़ता है।

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. But in snapping at the supposed treasure, he dropped the bit he was carrying, and so lost all. Sentences B. When he was crossing a river on his way home, he saw his own shadow reflected in the stream below. C. Thinking that it was another dog with another piece of meat, he resolved to make himself master of that also. D. A dog had stolen a piece of meat out of a butcher's shop
questions.explanation: C)DBCA D starts the paragraph by introducing the subject, "A dog," and the action it performed—"had stolen a piece of meat." This sets the context for the story. B follows D because it continues the narrative by describing the next action—"When he was crossing a river..." The pronoun "he" refers back to the dog introduced in D, maintaining subject continuity. C comes after B as it explains the dog’s reaction upon seeing his shadow—"Thinking that it was another dog..." The phrase "Thinking that" logically connects to the shadow observed in B, showing cause and effect. A concludes the paragraph by revealing the consequence of the dog’s action—"But in snapping at the supposed treasure, he dropped the bit he was carrying..." The word "But" contrasts the dog’s greed with the outcome, completing the moral of the story.Explanation in Hindi: D paragraph की शुरुआत करता है, क्योंकि यह subject "A dog" को introduce करता है और उसके द्वारा किए गए action ("had stolen a piece of meat") को बताता है। यह कहानी का context set करता है। B, D के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह narrative को आगे बढ़ाता है—"When he was crossing a river..." Pronoun "he", D में introduce किए गए dog को refer करता है, जिससे subject का flow बना रहता है। C, B के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह dog की reaction को explain करता है—"Thinking that it was another dog..." Phrase "Thinking that", B में देखे गए shadow से जुड़ता है, जो cause-effect relationship दिखाता है। A paragraph को conclude करता है, क्योंकि यह dog के action का result बताता है—"But in snapping at the supposed treasure, he dropped the bit he was carrying..." शब्द "But" dog के greed और उसके नतीजे के बीच contrast दिखाता है, जिससे moral पूरा होता है।

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. There is always something so fascinating about trains and journeys. There (1) ______ certain symbols and signages inside and outside of the train. The (2) ______ of most of them might not be known to everyone. (3) ______ you are a frequent traveller or not, you may have (4) ______ the letter X or a cross mark on the back of the last (5) ______ of the train. Q21. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: B) Are'का use होगा क्योंकि यह sentence वर्तमान समय के context में बता रहा है कि ट्रेनों में कुछ निशान होते हैं। Sentence में mention किया गया है कि ट्रेनों में और ट्रेनों के बाहर कुछ सिंबल और संकेत हैं, इसलिए 'are' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'were' भूतकाल में use होता है, 'had been' भूतकाल की समाप्त स्थितियों के लिए और 'will be' भविष्य के लिए use होता है, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। Are'should be used because the sentence is talking about a current, ongoing scenario, that there are certain symbols in and on trains. The sentence mentions that there are certain symbols and signages inside and outside of the train, making 'are' fitting here. Whereas, 'were' is used in past tense, 'had been' indicates a past completion and 'will be' is used for the future, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. There is always something so fascinating about trains and journeys. There (1) ______ certain symbols and signages inside and outside of the train. The (2) ______ of most of them might not be known to everyone. (3) ______ you are a frequent traveller or not, you may have (4) ______ the letter X or a cross mark on the back of the last (5) ______ of the train. Q22. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: D) Meanings'का use होगा क्योंकि यहाँ चर्चा संकेतों और प्रतीकों की हो रही है जिनका अर्थ आम लोगों को नहीं पता होता। sentence में बताया गया है कि अधिकतर संकेतों का अर्थ सभी को पता नहीं होता, इसलिए 'Meanings' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'senses' का अर्थ है अनुभूतियाँ, 'charge' का अर्थ है शुल्क या जिम्मेदारी, और 'worth' का अर्थ है मूल्य या अहमियत, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। Meanings'should be used because the passage discusses symbols and signs whose meanings might not be known to the general public. The sentence highlights that the meanings of most of these symbols are not known to everyone, making 'Meanings' appropriate here. Whereas, 'senses' implies perceptions or feelings, 'charge' means fee or responsibility, and 'worth' denotes value or importance, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. There is always something so fascinating about trains and journeys. There (1) ______ certain symbols and signages inside and outside of the train. The (2) ______ of most of them might not be known to everyone. (3) ______ you are a frequent traveller or not, you may have (4) ______ the letter X or a cross mark on the back of the last (5) ______ of the train. Q23. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: A) Whether'का use होगा क्योंकि यह शब्द दो संभावनाओं को दर्शाने के लिए प्रयुक्त होता है। Sentence में उल्लेख किया गया है कि आप अक्सर यात्रा करते हैं या नहीं, आपने ट्रेन की आखिरी डिब्बे पर X अक्षर या क्रॉस चिह्न को देखा हो सकता है, इसलिए 'Whether' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Whichever' और 'Whatever' विकल्प या चयन के संकेत के रूप में प्रयुक्त होते हैं, और 'However' विरोधाभास या संकेत दर्शाने के लिए प्रयुक्त होता है, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Whether'should be used as it introduces two possibilities. The sentence mentions that regardless of whether you frequently travel or not, you might have noticed the letter X or a cross mark on the back of the train's last compartment, making 'Whether' fitting here. On the other hand, 'Whichever' and 'Whatever' imply a choice or selection, and 'However' is used to indicate contrast or exception, which doesn't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. There is always something so fascinating about trains and journeys. There (1) ______ certain symbols and signages inside and outside of the train. The (2) ______ of most of them might not be known to everyone. (3) ______ you are a frequent traveller or not, you may have (4) ______ the letter X or a cross mark on the back of the last (5) ______ of the train. Q24. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: C) Noticed'का use होगा क्योंकि "noticed" का अर्थ होता है किसी विषय या चीज़ को देखना या समझना। Passage में इशारा किया गया है कि यात्री ट्रैन के आखिरी डिब्बे पर एक चिह्न को देख सकते हैं, इसलिए 'noticed' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Distinguished' का अर्थ है अलग पहचानना, 'Recognised' का अर्थ है पहचानना जिसे आप पहले देख चुके हैं, और 'Known' का अर्थ है जानते हैं, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Noticed'should be used because it means to observe or become aware of something. The passage hints that passengers may see a symbol on the back of the last car of the train, making 'noticed' the correct fit. Whereas, 'Distinguished' means to perceive as being different or distinct, 'Recognised' means to identify from having encountered it before, and 'Known' implies being familiar with, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. There is always something so fascinating about trains and journeys. There (1) ______ certain symbols and signages inside and outside of the train. The (2) ______ of most of them might not be known to everyone. (3) ______ you are a frequent traveller or not, you may have (4) ______ the letter X or a cross mark on the back of the last (5) ______ of the train. Q25. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: B) Coach'का use होगा क्योंकि "coach" का अर्थ होता है रेलगाड़ी का वह हिस्सा जिसमें यात्री सवार होते हैं। Sentence में mention किया गया है कि ट्रेन के अखिरी हिस्से पर X या cross mark होता है, इसलिए 'coach' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Booth' का अर्थ है एक छोटा कमरा या स्थान, 'Bus' एक प्रकार की वाहन है, और 'Rail' ट्रैक का हिस्सा होता है, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। Coach'should be used because it refers to the part of a train where passengers are seated. The sentence refers to a mark on the last part of the train, making 'coach' the most fitting choice. Whereas, 'Booth' refers to a small room or space, 'Bus' is a type of vehicle, and 'Rail' pertains to the track, which are not fitting in this context.
